creator: michael.schuh@gmail.com
text:
2012/11/6 Alexander Motin <mav@freebsd.org>:
> Hi.
>
> Enabling AHCI mode is quite messy process from the OS point of view, as it
> requires to change device memory allocation, that is not supposed to happen
> on that stage. I've decided to not do it. What we should do instead is find
> why legacy mode on your board is not working correctly. I guess problem is
> that your BIOS incorrectly setups device resource responsible for SATA
> registers access. Please provide parts of `pciconf -lvcb` output related to
> the controller and also full _verbose_ (press respective button in boot
> menu) dmesg output.
>
> --
> Alexander Motin

Yes, i can understand the reasons why not to change the controller modes.
Better a stable behaviour than a flaky mem alloc mess. That why i love
and always prefer FreeBSD over any other OS.

The funny part for me is: the kernel can see the SATA Channels, just
not the disks/drives connected. So it looked for me the ata stuff has
stopped device scanning after it found the channels.

Many thanks for dealing with this old kind of s... stuff. :-)

The requested pciconf -lvcb, i leaved it at once. I wasn't really sure
which parts are not needed. i made the earlier tests with and without
all of the plugged PCI-Cards.
